frameworks.name: Portilla-Simoncelli statistics
frameworks.one_line: image statistics from Gabor-like filter outputs and pairwise filter correlations

frameworks.name: linear-weighted encoding model
frameworks.one_line: optimally weighted sum of filter outputs mimics single-neuron responses

frameworks.name: hierarchical cortical processing
frameworks.one_line: natural scene analysis progresses from V1 to higher area V4

core_question: To what extent do Portilla-Simoncelli image statistics explain V1 and V4 neural responses to naturalistic videos, and how are tuning preferences spatially organized?
key_assumptions: natural scene responses can be captured by weighted sums of filter outputs and filter-correlation statistics
two-photon calcium imaging signals reflect tuning of individual neurons
Portilla-Simoncelli decomposition spans relevant low- to higher-order image statistics
manipulations: image-statistic class (luminance histogram, Gabor outputs, pairwise filter correlations across positions/orientations/scales)
cortical distance between neuron pairs at an imaging site
key_findings: encoding model reproduced responses in a subpopulation of V1 and V4 neurons
V1 neurons were predominantly driven by Gabor-like low-level statistics
V4 showed increased contribution of higher-order statistics such as cross-scale correlation
preferred image statistics varied across V4 sites and response similarity declined with cortical distance
interpretations: natural-scene analysis progresses hierarchically from V1 to V4
neurons sharing preferred image statistics are locally clustered in V4
